ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572); no specific Capricciosa subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category; no Capricciosa-specific code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Pizza Capricciosa found in published SNOMED CT release as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a whole 10–12 inch Pizza Capricciosa weighing approximately 400 g, providing around 1050 kcal, 55 g protein, 60 g fat, and 75 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat and vegetables, regular crust (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Pizza Capricciosa exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for pizza with meat and vegetable toppings on regular crust (FDC ID 172004 and related entries), cross-referenced with the input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). Input values are broadly consistent with USDA analogues and have been retained with minor rounding. Sodium at 625 mg/100 g is elevated relative to the USDA analogue (~550 mg/100 g) and reflects the ham and olive topping profile typical of Capricciosa. All values documented as adapted estimates.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for regular-crust pizza with cheese and meat toppings (GI range 60–65 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 46.9 g) / 100 = 28.1. Available carbohydrate per serving = 18.75 g/100 g × 400 g / 100 × (1 − fibre adjustment) ≈ 46.9 g net carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 625 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; a whole pizza delivers approximately 2500 mg sodium, approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit Pizza Capricciosa to one slice (approximately 80–100 g) and avoid additional salty condiments, as the ham, black olives, and mozzarella collectively drive sodium to 625 mg/100 g, which is directly associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined-flour crust contributes 18.75 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.25 g dietary fibre, yielding a medium GI of approximately 60 and a high glycemic load of 28 per whole pizza serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one to two slices, request a thin-crust or wholemeal base to reduce the glycemic load, and pair the meal with a large non-starchy vegetable salad to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 6.25 g/100 g and sodium at 625 mg/100 g, combined with processed meat toppings (ham), present a dual cardiovascular risk from LDL-raising saturated fat and blood-pressure-elevating sodium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should restrict Pizza Capricciosa to an occasional small portion (one slice), request removal of processed ham to reduce saturated fat and sodium, and choose a thin-crust version to lower the overall energy and saturated fat density of the meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Processed ham is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies inflammatory pathways; saturated fat at 6.25 g/100 g and sodium at 625 mg/100 g further worsen RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should request a version of Pizza Capricciosa with ham removed and toppings limited to artichoke hearts and mushrooms, which are anti-inflammatory, and monitor individual symptom response to tomato sauce as a nightshade-derived ingredient.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Capricciosa is a classic Italian pizza originating from Italy and widely served in Italian restaurants globally, including in Singapore. The name 'capricciosa' means 'capricious' or 'whimsical' in Italian, reflecting the varied combination of toppings. It is built on a round wheat-flour dough base spread with tomato sauce and topped with mozzarella cheese, artichoke hearts, sliced mushrooms, baked ham (prosciutto cotto), and black olives, then baked in a high-temperature oven until the crust is golden and the cheese is melted and lightly browned. The result is a pizza with a soft, slightly chewy crust, a savoury tomato and cheese base, and a complex topping profile that combines the earthiness of mushrooms and artichokes with the saltiness of ham and olives. Per 100 g, Pizza Capricciosa provides approximately 262.5 kcal, 13.75 g protein, 15.0 g fat (6.25 g saturated), 18.75 g carbohydrate, 1.25 g dietary fibre, and 625 mg sodium. The high sodium content (625 mg/100 g) is a primary clinical concern for hypertensive patients. The refined-flour crust produces a medium glycemic index of approximately 60 and a high glycemic load per whole pizza serving, making portion control important for patients with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and sodium combination also elevates cardiovascular risk. A standard serving is one whole 10–12 inch pizza weighing approximately 400 g.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry for Pizza Capricciosa exists. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for pizza with meat and vegetable toppings on regular crust, cross-referenced with the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The input values are broadly consistent with USDA analogues. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, fully documented. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adapted source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Pizza Capricciosa not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza entries as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy pizza analogue used for this record. Source consulted: USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/), accessed 2024-01-01. Sodium at 625 mg/100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g hypertension hard gate; health context flagged accordingly. The input record listed 'anchovies' and 'pork salami' in the health_suitability cautions but the canonical description and main_ingredients list for this record reflects the standard Capricciosa composition (artichokes, mushrooms, baked ham, black olives, tomato sauce, mozzarella) as stated in the input description; anchovies and salami are not included in this record's ingredient list. Halal status is false as baked ham (pork) is a standard topping. Two duplicate portion entries in the input (both 400 g, both is_default: true) have been consolidated to a single 400 g serving. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate; direct GI testing of Pizza Capricciosa was not found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024.
